meta.name: Tex-Mex Enchiladas (Red Sauce + Cheese + Chicken or Beef)
meta.subtitle: Saucy, cheesy baked enchiladas with a classic Tex-Mex chili-cumin profile; weeknight-friendly and scalable.

source.name: SSA Canonical Tex-Mex
notes: Built for deterministic planning: clear yields, equipment minimums, and optional components separated.
Ingredient IDs are SSA-friendly tokens; update to match your canonical ingredient catalog IDs as needed.

yield.notes: Makes 12 enchiladas (2 per serving). Use a 9x13 pan.

summary: Baked Tex-Mex enchiladas with a quick homemade red enchilada sauce, rolled tortillas, melty cheese, and a savory filling. Make ahead, freeze well, and reheat beautifully.

optional.label: Stand mixer
optional.notes: Useful for shredding cooked chicken quickly (paddle attachment).

ingredients.label: All-purpose flour
ingredients.notes: For gluten-free: use a 1:1 GF flour blend or cornstarch slurry (see variation).

ingredients.label: Chili powder
ingredients.notes: Use mild for kid-friendly; add cayenne if you want extra heat.

ingredients.label: Chicken broth (or vegetable broth)
ingredients.notes: Low-sodium preferred; adjust salt at the end.

ingredients.label: Kosher salt
ingredients.notes: Divided across sauce and filling; adjust to taste.

ingredients.label: Apple cider vinegar (or lime juice)
ingredients.notes: Brightens the sauce. Lime juice works well too.

ingredients.label: Cooked shredded chicken
ingredients.notes: Rotisserie chicken is perfect. Swap with cooked ground beef or beans (see variations).

ingredients.label: Diced green chiles (canned)
ingredients.notes: Mild heat and classic Tex-Mex flavor.

ingredients.label: Lime juice
ingredients.notes: Optional but recommended to brighten the filling.

ingredients.label: Tortillas (corn preferred; flour works too)
ingredients.notes: Corn tortillas are classic enchilada style. Flour tortillas roll easily but can get softer.

ingredients.label: Cheddar cheese, shredded
ingredients.notes: Swap with Colby Jack if preferred.

steps.title: Preheat oven + prep pan
instructions: Heat oven to 375°F (190°C).
Lightly oil a 9x13 baking dish or spray with cooking spray.

steps.title: Make quick red enchilada sauce
instructions: In a saucepan over medium heat, warm the oil.
Whisk in flour and cook 60–90 seconds until lightly toasty (do not brown too dark).
Whisk in chili powder, cumin, garlic powder, and oregano; cook 20–30 seconds to bloom.
Whisk in tomato paste until smooth.
Gradually whisk in broth. Bring to a gentle simmer, whisking until smooth and slightly thickened (3–5 minutes).
Season with salt and pepper, then stir in vinegar (or lime). Taste and adjust.
checks: Sauce coats a spoon lightly (not paste-thick)

steps.title: Cook aromatics + season filling
instructions: In a skillet over medium heat, add a small splash of oil if needed.
Cook diced onion until softened (3–4 minutes). Add minced garlic and cook 30 seconds.
Add shredded chicken and diced green chiles (optional).
Season with cumin, chili powder, and a pinch of salt/pepper. Toss to combine and warm through.
Finish with lime juice (optional). Taste and adjust seasoning.
checks: Filling tastes seasoned even before cheese/sauce
No raw garlic bite (only 30 seconds cooking needed)

steps.title: Soften tortillas for rolling
instructions: For corn tortillas: warm each tortilla in a dry skillet 15–20 seconds per side until pliable, OR microwave stacked under a damp paper towel for 30–45 seconds.
For flour tortillas: warm briefly in skillet or microwave just until flexible.
Keep warmed tortillas covered with a towel to stay soft.
checks: Stack kept covered to avoid drying

steps.title: Assemble enchiladas
instructions: Spread about 1/2 cup enchilada sauce in the bottom of the baking dish.
Fill each tortilla with ~1/4 cup chicken mixture and a small sprinkle of cheese, then roll snugly and place seam-side down in the dish.
Pour remaining sauce evenly over the rolled enchiladas.
Top with remaining cheese.

steps.title: Bake until bubbly
instructions: Bake at 375°F until the sauce is bubbling and cheese is fully melted, 18–22 minutes.
For more browning, broil 1–2 minutes at the end (watch closely).
Rest 5–10 minutes before serving to set.

steps.title: Serve + garnish
instructions: Top with chopped cilantro (optional).
Serve with sour cream and lime wedges if desired.
Pair with rice, beans, or a simple salad.

notes: Assemble enchiladas (without baking) up to 24 hours ahead; cover and refrigerate.
Add 5–10 minutes bake time if baking from cold.

fridge.notes: Cool, cover, and refrigerate. Reheat covered to keep moist.

notes: Freeze baked or unbaked. Wrap tightly (plastic + foil).
Thaw overnight in fridge if possible for best texture.

reheat: Oven: cover with foil, bake at 350°F for 20–30 minutes until hot.
Microwave: cover and heat in 60–90 second bursts until hot.

notes: If using chicken, ensure it is fully cooked before assembly (165°F / 74°C).

batchPrep: Double the sauce and freeze half for future enchiladas.
Shred chicken ahead (or use rotisserie) to reduce active time.

remixIdeas: Enchilada bowls (rice + chopped enchilada + extra sauce)
Stuffed baked potatoes topped with chopped enchilada

swap.notes: Use 1 lb ground beef browned and drained; season with the same spices.

notes: Add 1/2 cup drained black beans for stretch and fiber (optional).

swap.notes: Use ~3 cups cooked black beans; mash 1/3 for binding.

notes: Add sautéed bell peppers and corn to the filling if desired.
Use vegetable broth for the sauce.
Replace flour with 1 tbsp cornstarch slurry: whisk 1 tbsp cornstarch with 2 tbsp cold broth, then whisk into simmering sauce and cook 2–3 minutes.
Or use a 1:1 gluten-free flour blend in the roux.
Swap red sauce for salsa verde + a little broth to thin, then top with Monterey Jack.
Add extra lime and cilantro for brightness.
